title: Consistency Training Helps Stop Sycophancy and Jailbreaks
Definition of sycophancy: Models adopting user beliefs or opinions in response to prompt cues, compromising factual accuracy.
How they are measuring sycophancy: Susceptibility to leading questions and sycophantic prompt augmentations, measuring behavioral change across augmented vs. original prompts.
Tested methods for mitigating sycophancy: Bias-augmented Consistency Training (BCT) enforcing output invariance; Activation Consistency Training (ACT) enforcing internal activation invariance across prompt augmentations.
